# Automated Chart Updates

How an upstream dependency bump in an App repo (e.g. `giantswarm/kyverno-app`) is turned
into a dev-build, opened as an "automated update" PR in that App repo, and then
propagated into a single shared testing PR on `security-bundle` — kept in sync as CI
runs and torn down when the App-repo PR closes.

## Participating workflows

**App repo** (per-app, generated by `devctl` — `zz_generated.*`), each just calls a
reusable workflow in [`giantswarm/github-workflows`](https://github.com/giantswarm/github-workflows):

| App-repo workflow                              | Calls reusable workflow                        | Trigger                                   |
| ---------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------- |
| `zz_generated.sync_from_upstream.yaml`         | `sync-from-upstream.yaml`                       | push to `renovate/vendir/**`              |
| `zz_generated.update_chart.yaml`               | `update-chart.yaml`                             | push to `main#update-chart` / `workflow_call` |
| `zz_generated.dispatch_update_chart_events.yaml` | `dispatch-update-chart-events.yaml` / `dispatch-update-chart-pr-closed-events.yaml` | push to `main#update-chart`; PR `closed` |

**`security-bundle`** (central — handles the three `repository_dispatch` events):

| Dispatch event           | Workflow                          | Purpose                                                  |
| ------------------------ | --------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------- |
| `update-chart`           | `automated_chart_update.yaml`     | Point `values.yaml` at the new dev-build; open/update the shared PR |
| `update-chart-status`    | `chart_status_update.yaml`        | Patch the per-app status cell (⏳ / ✅ / ❌) in the PR body |
| `update-chart-pr-closed` | `automated_chart_pr_closed.yaml`  | Revert the app and drop its row when the App-repo PR closes |

The central PR body table is maintained by `hack/update_pr_body.py`. All workflows
authenticate as the **`HeraldBot[bot]`** GitHub App (`HERALD_CLIENT_ID` / `HERALD_APP_KEY`);
the App-repo PR itself is pushed by `taylorbot` (`TAYLORBOT_GITHUB_ACTION`).

---

## 1. End-to-end overview

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    subgraph app["App repo — e.g. giantswarm/kyverno-app"]
        renovate["<b>Renovate</b> bumps a pinned upstream dep<br/>opens branch <code>renovate/vendir/**</code>"]

        subgraph sync["sync_from_upstream → sync-from-upstream.yaml"]
            prep["prepare-update-branch:<br/>create/update branch <code>main#update-chart</code>,<br/>copy <code>vendir.yml</code> from renovate branch"]
            upd["call-update-chart → update-chart.yaml:<br/><code>make update-chart</code> (vendir sync),<br/>commit as taylorbot,<br/><b>open App-repo PR</b> (head <code>main#update-chart</code> → <code>main</code>)"]
            synced["sync-and-update:<br/>values-sync + schema-gen + changelogger,<br/>commit & push to <code>main#update-chart</code>"]
        end

        ci["App CI builds dev chart<br/>gitsemver tag <code>-dev.&lt;branch&gt;.&lt;datetime&gt;.h&lt;sha&gt;</code><br/>→ pushes to OCI <code>gsoci.azurecr.io/charts/giantswarm/&lt;app&gt;</code>"]

        subgraph disp["dispatch_update_chart_events"]
            dispmain["on push to <code>main#update-chart</code> →<br/>dispatch-update-chart-events.yaml"]
            dispclosed["on PR <code>closed</code> →<br/>dispatch-update-chart-pr-closed-events.yaml"]
        end
    end

    subgraph sb["security-bundle (central)"]
        wf_update["automated_chart_update.yaml"]
        wf_status["chart_status_update.yaml"]
        wf_closed["automated_chart_pr_closed.yaml"]
        branch["branch <code>automated-chart-updates</code><br/>+ one shared PR to <code>main</code>"]
    end

    renovate -->|push| prep --> upd --> synced
    synced -->|push to main#update-chart| ci
    synced -->|push to main#update-chart| dispmain

    dispmain -->|"repository_dispatch<br/>update-chart"| wf_update
    dispmain -.->|"waits for App CI,<br/>then update-chart-status"| wf_status
    ci -.->|chart available for lookup| wf_update
    dispclosed -->|"repository_dispatch<br/>update-chart-pr-closed"| wf_closed

    wf_update -->|"set version + catalog (-test),<br/>commit, open/update PR, add row ⏳"| branch
    wf_status -->|"patch status cell ⏳→✅/❌"| branch
    wf_closed -->|"revert version + catalog,<br/>remove row"| branch
```

---

## 2. App-repo side — from Renovate to the dispatch

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    autonumber
    participant Ren as Renovate
    participant Sync as sync-from-upstream.yaml
    participant UpdPR as update-chart.yaml
    participant Branch as branch main#update-chart
    participant AppPR as App-repo PR
    participant CI as App CI (build & push chart)
    participant Disp as dispatch-update-chart-events.yaml
    participant SB as security-bundle

    Ren->>Branch: open renovate/vendir/** (bump vendir.yml)
    Note over Sync: triggered by push to renovate/vendir/**
    Sync->>Branch: create/update main#update-chart,<br/>copy vendir.yml onto it
    Sync->>UpdPR: call-update-chart
    UpdPR->>Branch: make update-chart (vendir sync), commit (taylorbot)
    UpdPR->>AppPR: create PR "chore(chart): automated update from upstream"<br/>(head main#update-chart → main, label automated-update)
    Sync->>Branch: values-sync + schema-gen + changelogger,<br/>commit & push
    Sync->>AppPR: update PR description (changes + values drift)

    Note over CI: same push builds the dev chart
    CI->>CI: gitsemver dev-build tag, push to OCI registry

    Note over Disp: also triggered by push to main#update-chart
    Disp->>Disp: derive commit_datetime + sanitized branch (gitsemver ids)
    Disp->>SB: repository_dispatch update-chart<br/>{repo, sha, branch, commit_datetime}
    Disp->>CI: poll commit checks (up to 20 min)
    Disp->>SB: repository_dispatch update-chart-status<br/>{..., status: waiting|success|failure}
```

Key points on the App side:

- **Two branches.** Renovate's `renovate/vendir/**` branch is only the *trigger*. The
  actual update happens on a dedicated `main#update-chart` branch, and the App-repo PR is
  opened from `main#update-chart` → `main`. (The `#` lets `update-chart.yaml` derive the
  base branch as `main`.)
- **The dev chart is built by normal App CI** on the push to `main#update-chart`, tagged
  with a gitsemver dev-build version and pushed to
  `gsoci.azurecr.io/charts/giantswarm/<app>`.
- **The dispatch workflow waits** for the App's CI checks (default 20 min) before sending
  `update-chart-status`, so the central PR reflects whether the dev build actually passed.

---

## 3. security-bundle side — the shared testing PR

Every app's dispatch converges on the single `automated-chart-updates` branch and the one
PR opened from it (`test(apps): [DO NOT MERGE] Update apps testing versions`). The PR body
holds one table row per app/build.

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    autonumber
    participant App as App repo dispatch
    participant OCI as OCI registry
    participant Update as automated_chart_update.yaml
    participant Status as chart_status_update.yaml
    participant Repo as automated-chart-updates branch
    participant PR as security-bundle PR

    App->>Update: update-chart {repo, sha, branch, commit_datetime}
    Note over Update: validate inputs,<br/>derive app_name + values.yaml key
    Update->>Repo: create / checkout automated-chart-updates
    Update->>OCI: crane ls — find matching dev tag<br/>(retry up to 40× / 10 min)
    alt tag found
        Update->>Repo: set version + switch catalog to "…-test", commit & push
        Update->>PR: create PR (or edit body) — add row, status ⏳ Waiting
    else tag never appears
        Update->>PR: add row with status ❌ Failed
    end

    App->>Status: update-chart-status {..., status}
    Note over Status: poll PR body up to 10× / 5 min<br/>until app+datetime row exists
    Status->>PR: patch status cell → ⏳ / ✅ / ❌
```

Key points on the central side:

- **Catalog is switched to `-test`** so the bundle pulls the dev chart, not the released one.
- **Tag resolution is best-effort:** waits up to 10 min for the dev tag to appear in OCI,
  matching the `branch.datetime` suffix, falling back to the short-SHA suffix.
- **The PR is shared** across all apps; rows are added, status-patched, and removed
  independently per app.

### PR body modes (`hack/update_pr_body.py`)

```mermaid
flowchart LR
    in["existing PR body (stdin)"] --> mode{which mode?}
    mode -->|"NEW_VERSION set"| full["Full update<br/>insert/replace row<br/>default status ⏳"]
    mode -->|"REMOVE set"| rm["Remove<br/>drop row for app<br/>(optionally by datetime)"]
    mode -->|"only STATUS set"| st["Status-only<br/>patch status cell"]
    full --> out["new PR body (stdout)"]
    rm --> out
    st --> out
```

---

## 4. Cleanup — App-repo PR closed

When the `main#update-chart` PR in the App repo is closed (merged or abandoned),
`dispatch-update-chart-pr-closed-events.yaml` fires and dispatches `update-chart-pr-closed`
to security-bundle, which undoes just that app's entry.

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    autonumber
    participant App as App repo (PR closed)
    participant Closed as automated_chart_pr_closed.yaml
    participant Repo as automated-chart-updates branch
    participant PR as security-bundle PR

    App->>Closed: update-chart-pr-closed {repo}
    alt branch automated-chart-updates exists
        Closed->>Repo: read main's values.yaml,<br/>restore version + catalog
        Closed->>Repo: commit "Revert the app ..." & push
        Closed->>PR: remove the app's row from body
    else no branch
        Note over Closed: nothing to do, skip
    end
```

There is no auto-close step on the central PR — once the table is empty (or testing is
done), it is merged or closed manually.

---

## Notes

- App-repo workflows are `devctl`-generated (`zz_generated.*`) and only call the reusable
  workflows in `giantswarm/github-workflows`; the real logic lives there.
- All three central workflows also support manual `workflow_dispatch` with the same inputs,
  useful for replaying or debugging a single app.
